methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
WB Group is a pre-IPO stage Defense & Security company founded by Piotr Wojciechowski. The valuation exercise draws from a 10-company comparable universe consisting of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private competitors including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 20 same-sector peers in Defense & Security, strict filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in any subcategory—the minimum threshold for meaningful triangulation. A sector-wide median would introduce material noise given the heterogeneity in business model, maturity, and capital efficiency across Defense & Security companies at different stages. As a result, this method is excluded from synthesis.

Without viable peer-median support, the analysis cannot reliably triangulate to a recommended valuation range. The core constraint is the scarcity of sufficiently comparable pre-IPO stage peers with publicly disclosed valuations. To advance a valuation, additional methods (DCF, precedent transactions, or analogous late-stage fundraises) would be required. Key uncertainties include WB Group's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and actual raise history—all material to risk-adjusted valuation but not provided in available data.
